title: Constants, Literals, Domain Types, and Configuration · Gate Forensics
summary: Replace ambiguous magic values without creating meaningless constant noise. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a domain constant and configuration policy and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish harmless local literals from repeated domain rules, units, limits, and protocol values.
Name stable domain values by meaning and keep them near their owning policy.
Use enums, unions, literal types, branded values, or value objects for closed domains.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record
steps.instruction:
Use a changed debug case derived from this module: gates constants literals. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Inspect the Literal Risk trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish harmless local literals from repeated domain rules, units, limits, and protocol values.

content.text: For Literal Risk, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: Name the Literal Risk precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.
Trace this concrete case before choosing: Use a changed debug case derived from this module: gates constants literals. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Every numeric or string literal must become a constant.

stimulus.title: Literal Risk case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Use a changed debug case derived from this module: gates constants literals. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Every numeric or string literal must become a constant.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish harmless local literals from repeated domain rules, units, limits, and protocol values. Artifact under review: a domain constant and configuration policy.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Literal Risk, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a gates-constant-tests-1eglln: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Constant Tests into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
gates-constant-tests-1eglln:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

steps.title: Order the regression proof
steps.instruction: Order the Constant Tests evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Constant Tests assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Constant Tests applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Test boundary values and downstream behavior instead of only constant equality.
